title: Bitter Chewy Turkish Plain Flour Turkish Flatbread Pide
description: Turkish flatbread that is perfect for a midnight craving, with a bitter taste and chewy texture. Try it with a dollop of yogurt or a sprinkle of sumac.

ingredients.name: Namul-blanch-season
ingredients.notes: Blended from herbs and spices, this seasoning gives the flatbread a unique flavor.

ingredients.name: Sugar
ingredients.notes: Balances out the bitterness of the namul-blanch-season.

ingredients.name: Ghee or Butter
ingredients.notes: Optional, but adds a rich flavor to the flatbread.

ingredients.name: Yogurt
ingredients.notes: Optional, adds a dollop of creamy texture.

instructions: Combine plain flour, baking soda, salt, sugar, and namul-blanch-season in a large mixing bowl.
Add water to the bowl and mix until the dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl and let it rise in a warm place for 1-2 hours.
Preheat the oven to 220 degree Celsius.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal portions depending on the size of flatbread you want to make.
Shape each portion into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Place the flatbreads on a preheated baking stone or baking tray, leaving space between them for even cooking.
Drizzle with ghee or butter and sprinkle with yogurt and sumac if using.
Bake the flatbreads in the preheated oven for 10-15 minutes or until they are golden brown.
Remove the flatbreads from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
